exemplar.title:

affect Kalimantanâs orangutan conservation and its potential economic impacts/
implications

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does Indonesiaâs proposed plan of moving the capital city to IKN
affect Kalimantanâs orangutan conservation and its potential economic impacts/
implications

strengths: Topic and global issue are communicated clearly and succinctly through a discipline-appropriate title
Consistent reference to thematic lenses (biology and economics) maintains focus throughout
Research question is explicitly stated and framed to guide direction
weaknesses: Research question combines two broad dimensions, diluting sharp focus
Methodology justification lacks detail on survey design, sampling and handling of failed interviews
Purpose statement is wordy and could be distilled into more concise objectives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission excels in clearly identifying and communicating the interdisciplinary topic, warranting a score in the upper half of the 0â6 range. The research question is well-formulated but bundles two substantial dimensions, and the methodological justification is only partially developed. Together, these place the work above the mid-level band but short of the very highest band, justifying a score of 5.

strengths: A broad range of roughly 30 mostly pertinent academic, NGO and government sources
Accurate and consistent use of key biological and economic terminology
Clear demonstration of core knowledge in both orangutan ecology and economic theory
weaknesses: A few popular news sources weaken overall academic rigor
Depth of integration between disciplines remains descriptive in parts
Source selection criteria and rationale are not explicitly justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source selection is generally relevant and terminology use is accurate, placing the work solidly in the mid-range of 0â6. Knowledge application is clear but only moderately integrative, and weaknesses in source justification and occasional descriptive passages prevent a higher band, leading to a score of 4.

strengths: Rigorous comparative case-study analysis (e.g., Brasilia vs. IKN) shows clear interdisciplinary synthesis
Interlinking of ecological welfare loss with economic externalities demonstrates critical insight
Findings are largely supported by coherent analysis of survey, literature and statistical data
weaknesses: Evaluation of data biases (NGO, government sources) is uneven
Counter-arguments and contextual limitations receive only brief treatment
Some conclusions lack the nuanced depth required for the very top band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation displays coherent, interdisciplinary argument structure and well-supported analysis that surpasses basic evaluation, warranting placement in the 7â9 band of the 0â12 range. Occasional unevenness in bias evaluation and limited exploration of counter-perspectives precludes the highest band, so a score of 7 reflects the strong but not fully distinguished critical thinking.

strengths: Clear academic structure with a well-organized table of contents and hierarchical headings
Relevant visuals (graphs and charts) support the analysis
In-text citations and reference list adhere broadly to APA conventions
weaknesses: Visuals are low resolution and captions often minimal
Formatting inconsistencies in citations (line breaks in URLs, missing years)
Sections occasionally truncate mid-argument and long paragraphs impede readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Academic conventions are followed coherently, and visuals add value, but persistent formatting inconsistencies and readability issues limit the work to the mid-range of the 0â4 criterion. These strengths and weaknesses combine to justify a score of 3.

weaknesses: No RPPF or reflective documentation is provided
No evidence of personal engagement or reflective analysis
Intellectual initiative and future research proposals are absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no reflective portfolio or documentation of personal and intellectual engagement, the submission fails to meet any descriptors for this criterion, resulting in a score of 0.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows a logical section structure and attempts interdisciplinary synthesis (biology â economics).  Some critical insight appears (trade-offs, externalities), yet evaluation of alternative perspectives is brief, so argument coherence is solid but not nuanced.

A reference list in approximate APA style and in-text citations are present throughout.  Formatting inconsistencies (line breaks in URLs, occasional missing years) prevent full consistency but overall adherence to academic conventions is evident.

The student states a concise, discipline-appropriate title and immediately clarifies the global issue (orangutan conservation vs. economic development) and the disciplinary lenses (biology + economics). Throughout the paper the stated focus is consistently referred to, indicating a clear and appropriate communication of topic.
A table of contents, hierarchical headings and conventional introduction-body-conclusion format create a clear academic structure that guides the reader effectively.

The essay demonstrates sound knowledge of orangutan ecology and economic theory (externalities, demand shifts).  Application is clear in sections, but some parts remain descriptive and lack depth of integration; therefore sources are used with moderate effectiveness.
No RPPF or other reflective documentation is included, so evidence of reflection on planning or skills is absent.

Analysis links survey results, case-study comparison and ecological data to the question, producing largely supported conclusions.  However, evaluation of limitations and counter-arguments is uneven, preventing the incisiveness required for the top bands.

The research question is explicitly given and framed as âTo what extentâ¦,â signalling direction; however, it bundles two substantial dimensions (conservation impact and economic implications).  This dual aim renders the focus only partially sharp, so a secure rather than full focus is demonstrated.

The bulk of the research stems from suitable journal articles, NGO data and official statistics, with few off-topic inclusions.  While selection is not consistently rigorous, the majority of evidence employed is appropriate and well aligned to the investigation.
Without the RPPF, intellectual initiative (original ideas for further inquiry, adaptation of methods) cannot be evidenced and must be marked as absent.

Methods include secondary literature, a small-scale survey, and an attempted expert interview.  The choices are generally appropriate, yet justification is limited (e.g., survey design and sampling not explained; failed interviews left unmitigated).  The selection therefore shows some informed thought but is not fully effective.

Roughly 30 sources spanning academic papers, NGO reports and government documents are cited.  Most are directly pertinent to either orangutans or IKN economics, indicating generally relevant selection, although a few popular news items weaken overall rigour.
Because reflections are missing, there is no demonstration of personal engagement with the research process.

Hand-drawn supply/demand graphs and pie charts are relevant and labelled, though captions are sometimes minimal and resolution is low.  Overall the visuals are acceptable and support arguments, but presentation quality is not exemplary.

Key biological and economic terminology (habitat fragmentation, negative externality, supply curve) is accurately defined and used consistently.  Conceptual use is mostly correct, though not especially sophisticated, fitting solid but not expert command.

annotations.comment: Charts are low-resolution and hard to read; replace with higher-quality graphics.
Area of study and thematic lenses are clearly stated, strengthening disciplinary focus for biology and economics.
The declared word count (3852) falls comfortably under the 4000-word limit, satisfying formatting requirements.
List and justify criteria for selecting each class of secondary source to strengthen source relevance.
The title succinctly captures the interdisciplinary focus and global issue, providing clear communication of topic.
Bundling conservation and economic impacts in one question dilutes focus; consider narrowing to a single primary dimension or splitting into sub-questions.
Table of contents is well organized with hierarchical headings, guiding the reader effectively through the essayâs structure.
Page numbers appear consistently in the footer, meeting the requirement for numbered pages.
Indentation of sub-sections in the TOC is uneven; align entry spacing to clearly distinguish levels.
Background context is thorough but leans descriptive; integrate theoretical frameworks to deepen analysis.
Phrasing in the purpose statement is wordy; distill goals into concise objectives to strengthen alignment with the research question.
Methodology lacks detail on survey sampling and interview response rates; justify source choices and address non-responses.
In-text citations mix plain text and math tags; revise to a consistent APA style without MathML artifacts.
Include critical evaluation of NGO and government data biases when discussing ecotourism potential.
The economic explanation of diversification away from palm oil is clear and uses terminology accurately.
Section on demand assessment is truncated mid-sentence; ensure the full argument is presented.
Survey sampling frame (mostly Java residents) introduces bias; discuss limitations and potential corrective measures.
Figures lack detailed captions and axis labels; add descriptive titles and units for clarity.
Survey percentage results lack sample size (n); include respondent count to contextualize percentages.
Supply-demand shift described correctly, but variables should be formatted in LaTeX (e.g., $$Q_e$$) in the text.
Break up the long case study paragraphs into focused sections to improve readability and logical flow.
Comparative analysis with Brasilia is well developed, showing thoughtful interdisciplinary synthesis.
Paragraph ends abruptly mid-argument; ensure complete presentation of counter-arguments and balancing points.
Graphâs axes lack labels; add titles and units for supply and quantity to clarify reading.
Interdisciplinary linkage between economic theory and ecological welfare loss is insightful and well supported.
Brazil to IKN extrapolation needs caution; discuss contextual differences in governance and geography.
Use of ecological terminology (e.g., âkeystone speciesâ) is accurate and enhances clarity.
Quantitative deforestation figures are compelling; place citations immediately after each data point for traceability.
Define quantitative fragmentation thresholds to strengthen the discussion of habitat isolation impacts.
Figure 4 lacks a clear caption placement and legend; position the title directly below and include data series labels.
Conclusion effectively summarizes findings but is repetitive; tighten language and focus key insights.
Reference list exhibits inconsistent URL line breaks and missing years for some sources; standardize APA formatting.
Several references use ân.d.â; seeking publication dates or annotating rationale would enhance source credibility.
List items in later pages extend beyond references; remove extraneous appendix content to maintain focus.
